articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal! VfB Stuttgart 1, Borussia Dortmund 0. Deniz Undav (VfB Stuttgart) left footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Maximilian Mittelstädt.
Jamie Leweling (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card.
Goal! VfB Stuttgart 2, Borussia Dortmund 0. Ermedin Demirovic (VfB Stuttgart) header from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Maximilian Mittelstädt with a cross.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Jamie Gittens replaces Felix Nmecha because of an injury.
First Half ends, VfB Stuttgart 2, Borussia Dortmund 0.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Yan Couto replaces Julian Ryerson.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Ramy Bensebaini replaces Karim Adeyemi.
Second Half begins VfB Stuttgart 2, Borussia Dortmund 0.

Goal! VfB Stuttgart 3, Borussia Dortmund 0. Enzo Millot (VfB Stuttgart) left footed shot from the left side of the six yard box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Atakan Karazor with a headed pass following a corner.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Donyell Malen replaces Pascal Groß.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Fabian Rieder replaces Ermedin Demirovic.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Dan-Axel Zagadou replaces Jeff Chabot.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Anrie Chase replaces Maximilian Mittelstädt.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Emre Can replaces Julian Brandt.
Emre Can (Borussia Dortmund) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.

Goal! VfB Stuttgart 3, Borussia Dortmund 1. Serhou Guirassy (Borussia Dortmund) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Jamie Gittens following a fast break.
Marcel Sabitzer (Borussia Dortmund) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. El Bilal Touré replaces Josha Vagnoman.
Goal! VfB Stuttgart 4, Borussia Dortmund 1. El Bilal Touré (VfB Stuttgart) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Enzo Millot.
Ramy Bensebaini (Borussia Dortmund) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Chris Führich replaces Angelo Stiller.
Goal! VfB Stuttgart 5, Borussia Dortmund 1. Deniz Undav (VfB Stuttgart) left footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Enzo Millot following a fast break.

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (VfB Stuttgart vs. Borussia Dortmund - Game Highlights) 2024/09/22 ESHEET

article.story:
Deniz Undav scored a brace as a rampant VfB Stuttgart put on a spectacular display to shock Borussia Dortmund 5-1 at home on Sunday, handing Nuri Sahin's side their first defeat of the Bundesliga season.

Eighth-placed Dortmund are level on seven points with Stuttgart, who sit one place higher thanks to a better goal difference, ahead of Sunday's remaining fixtures.

Undav got Stuttgart off to a flying start inside five minutes at the MHP Arena when the forward found left back Maximilian Mittelstadt's pass, stormed into the box and fired the ball into the far bottom corner.

He nearly made it 2-0 within seconds to continue his goal-scoring form, which has seen him score five goals across all competitions this season with the latest coming in their 3-1 defeat at Real Madrid in the Champions League, but he failed to time his shot.

Last season's runners-up tore apart the stunned visitors' defence, with Mittelstadt providing Ermedin Demirovic with a chipped cross to head home and double their lead in the 21st minute. It was Demirovic's second successive league goal.

The hosts piled on the pressure and carved out several chances, with Dortmund goalkeeper Gregor Kobel making a stellar double save to deny Frenchman Enzo Millot's powerful volley from distance and Undav on the rebound.

But Mittelstadt's short corner found Atakan Karazor, who headed it back across the goal for Millot to tap in from close range and make it 3-0 for Stuttgart in the 62nd minute.

Dortmund did not pose a challenge to Sebastian Hoeness' Stuttgart, who were sharper and more aggressive.

Former Stuttgart man Serhou Guirassy reduced Dortmund's deficit with a goal in the 72nd minute, but substitute El Bilal Toure and Undav made matters worse as they added another goal each to Stuttgart's tally to make it 5-1.

Stuttgart had beaten Dortmund home and away in the Bundesliga last season as well as the German Cup.
